result.name: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux

nvt.name: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=The Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty version on the remote host has reached the End of Life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.|insight=|affected=|impact=An EOL version of Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unfixed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.|solution=Update the Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty version on the remote host to a still supported version.|vuldetect=Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update the Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty version on the remote host to a still supported version.

result.description:
The "Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty" version on the remote host has reached the end of life.
CPE: cpe:/a:eclipse:jetty:9.2.13.20150730 Installed version: 9.2.13.20150730 Location/URL: 8161/tcp EOL version: 9.2 EOL date: 2018-12-31

result.name: Apache ActiveMQ Web Console Default / No Credentials (HTTP)

nvt.name: Apache ActiveMQ Web Console Default / No Credentials (HTTP)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=The Apache ActiveMQ Web Console is unprotected or is using default credentials.|insight=|affected=|impact=This issue may be exploited by a remote attacker to gain access to sensitive information.|solution=Change or set the password.|vuldetect=|solution_type=Mitigation

result.description:

http://activemq514.trabalho_vulnnet:8161/admin/index.jsp using user "admin" with password "admin"
http://activemq514.trabalho_vulnnet:8161/admin/index.jsp using user "user" with password "user"

result.name: Eclipse Jetty Server Fake Pipeline Request Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Eclipse Jetty Server Fake Pipeline Request Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Eclipse Jetty Server is prone to a security bypass vulnerability.|insight=The flaw exists due to an improper validation against pipelined requests.|affected=Eclipse Jetty Server versions 9.2.x before 9.2.25.v20180606, 9.3.x before 9.3.24.v20180605 and 9.4.x before 9.4.11.v20180605.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow an attacker to bypass authorization.|solution=Upgrade to Eclipse Jetty Server version 9.2.25.v20180606 or 9.3.24.v20180605 or 9.4.11.v20180605 or later as per the series. Please see the references for more information.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to Eclipse Jetty Server version 9.2.25.v20180606 or 9.3.24.v20180605 or 9.4.11.v20180605 or later as per the series. Please see the references for more information.

result.description: Installed version: 9.2.13.20150730 Fixed version: 9.2.25.v20180606 Installation path / port: 8161/tcp

result.name: HTTP Brute Force Logins With Default Credentials Reporting

nvt.name: HTTP Brute Force Logins With Default Credentials Reporting
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=It was possible to login into the remote Web Application using default credentials.|insight=As the VT 'HTTP Brute Force Logins With Default Credentials' (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.108041) might run into a timeout the actual reporting of this vulnerability takes place in this VT instead.|affected=|impact=This issue may be exploited by a remote attacker to e.g. gain access to sensitive information or modify system configuration.|solution=Change the password as soon as possible.|vuldetect=Reports default credentials detected by the VT 'HTTP Brute Force Logins With Default Credentials' (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.108041).|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution: Change the password as soon as possible.

result.name: Eclipse Jetty DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-26vr-8j45-3r4w) - Linux

nvt.name: Eclipse Jetty DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-26vr-8j45-3r4w) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Eclipse Jetty is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=|affected=Eclipse Jetty version 7.2.2 through 9.4.38, 10.0.0.alpha0 through 10.0.1 and 11.0.0.alpha0 through 11.0.1.|impact=When using SSL/TLS with Jetty, either with HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, or WebSocket, the server may receive an invalid large (greater than 17408) TLS frame that is incorrectly handled, causing CPU resources to eventually reach 100% usage.|solution=Update to version 9.4.39, 10.0.2, 11.0.2 or later. See the referenced vendor advisory for a possible mitigation.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 9.4.39, 10.0.2, 11.0.2 or later. See the referenced vendor advisory for a possible mitigation.

result.description: Installed version: 9.2.13.20150730 Fixed version: 9.4.39 Installation path / port: 8161/tcp

- CVE-2022-2048: Invalid HTTP/2 requests can lead to denial of service|affected=Eclipse Jetty version 9.4.46 and prior, version 10.0.x through 10.0.9 and 11.0.x through 11.0.9.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.4.47, 10.0.10, 11.0.10 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Eclipse Jetty DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-7hcf-ppf8-5w5h) - Linux

nvt.name: Eclipse Jetty DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-7hcf-ppf8-5w5h) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Eclipse Jetty is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=There exists a security vulnerability in Jetty's DosFilter which can be exploited by unauthorized users to cause remote denial of service (DoS) attack on the server using DosFilter. By repeatedly sending crafted requests, attackers can trigger OutofMemory errors and exhaust the server's memory finally.|affected=Eclipse Jetty version 9.0.0 through 9.4.53, 10.0.0 through 10.0.17, 11.0.0 through 11.0.17 and 12.0.0 through 12.0.2.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.4.54, 10.0.18, 11.0.18, 12.0.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.2.13.20150730 Fixed version: 9.4.54 Installation path / port: 8161/tcp

result.name: Eclipse Jetty Privilege Escalation Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Eclipse Jetty Privilege Escalation Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Eclipse Jetty is prone to a privilege escalation vulnerability through a local temp directory.|insight=On Unix like systems, the system's temporary directory is shared between all users on that system. A collocated user can observe the process of creating a temporary sub directory in the shared temporary directory and race to complete the creation of the temporary subdirectory. If the attacker wins the race then they will have read and write permission to the subdirectory used to unpack web applications, including their WEB-INF/lib jar files and JSP files. If any code is ever executed out of this temporary directory, this can lead to a local privilege escalation vulnerability.|affected=Eclipse Jetty version 9.4.32.v20200930 and prior, 10.0.0.beta2 and prior and 11.0.0.beta2 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.4.33.v20201020, 10.0.0.beta3, 11.0.0.beta3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.2.13.20150730 Fixed version: 9.4.33.20201020 Installation path / port: 8161/tcp

result.name: MQTT Broker Does Not Require Authentication (TCP)

nvt.name: MQTT Broker Does Not Require Authentication (TCP)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:N|summary=The remote MQTT broker does not require authentication.|insight=|affected=|impact=This issue may be exploited by a remote attacker to gain access to sensitive information.|solution=Enable authentication.|vuldetect=Checks if authentication is required for the remote MQTT broker.
Notes:
- No scan result is expected if localhost (127.0.0.1) was scanned (self scanning)
- If the scanned network is e.g. a private LAN / private WAN which contains systems not accessible to the public (access restricted) and it is accepted that the service is accessible without authentication please set the 'Network type' configuration of the following VT to e.g. 'Private LAN' or 'Private WAN':
Global variable settings (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.12288)|solution_type=Mitigation

result.name: Eclipse Jetty Information Disclosure Vulnerability (GHSA-gwcr-j4wh-j3cq)

nvt.name: Eclipse Jetty Information Disclosure Vulnerability (GHSA-gwcr-j4wh-j3cq)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N|summary=Eclipse Jetty is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability in the ConcatServlet and WelcomeFilter servlet.|insight=Requests to the ConcatServlet and WelcomeFilter are able to access protected resources within the WEB-INF directory. For example a request to the ConcatServlet with a URI of /concat?/%2557EB-INF/web.xml can retrieve the web.xml file. This can reveal sensitive information regarding the implementation of a web application.
This occurs because both ConcatServlet and WelcomeFilter decode the supplied path to verify it is not within the WEB-INF or META-INF directories. It then uses this decoded path to call RequestDispatcher which will also do decoding of the path. This double decoding allows paths with a doubly encoded WEB-INF to bypass this security check.|affected=Eclipse Jetty prior to version 9.4.41, 10.x through 10.0.2 and 11.x through 11.0.2.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.4.41, 10.0.3, 11.0.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.2.13.20150730 Fixed version: 9.4.41 Installation path / port: 8161/tcp

nvt.name: Eclipse Jetty Multiple Vulnerabilities (GHSA-qw69-rqj8-6qw8, GHSA-p26g-97m4-6q7c) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N|summary=Eclipse Jetty is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2023-26048: OutOfMemoryError for large multipart without filename read via request.getParameter()
- CVE-2023-26049: Cookie parsing of quoted values can exfiltrate values from other cookies|affected=Eclipse Jetty version 9.4.50 and prior, version 10.0.x through 10.0.13 and 11.0.x through 11.0.13.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.4.51, 10.0.14, 11.0.14 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Eclipse Jetty URI Parsing Vulnerability (GHSA-qh8g-58pp-2wxh) - Linux

nvt.name: Eclipse Jetty URI Parsing Vulnerability (GHSA-qh8g-58pp-2wxh) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N|summary=Eclipse Jetty is prone to an URI parsing vulnerability.|insight=Eclipse Jetty includes a utility class, HttpURI, for URI/URL parsing.
The HttpURI class does insufficient validation on the authority segment of a URI. However the behaviour of HttpURI differs from the common browsers in how it handles a URI that would be considered invalid if fully validated against the RRC. Specifically HttpURI and the browser may differ on the value of the host extracted from an invalid URI and thus a combination of Jetty and a vulnerable browser may be vulnerable to a open redirect attack or to a SSRF attack if the URI is used after passing validation checks.|affected=Eclipse Jetty version 7.0.0 through 12.0.11.|impact=|solution=Update to version 12.0.12 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.2.13.20150730 Fixed version: 12.0.12 Installation path / port: 8161/tcp

nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N|summary=Eclipse Jetty Server is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.|insight=The flaw exists due to an improper handling of bad queries.|affected=Eclipse Jetty Server versions 9.2.x, 9.3.x before 9.3.24.v20180605 and 9.4.x before 9.4.11.v20180605.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow an attacker to disclose sensitive information.|solution=Upgrade to Eclipse Jetty Server version 9.3.24.v20180605 or 9.4.11.v20180605 or later as per the series. Please see the references for more information.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to Eclipse Jetty Server version 9.3.24.v20180605 or 9.4.11.v20180605 or later as per the series. Please see the references for more information.

result.name: Eclipse Jetty HTTP Header Vulnerability (GHSA-hmr7-m48g-48f6) - Linux

nvt.name: Eclipse Jetty HTTP Header Vulnerability (GHSA-hmr7-m48g-48f6) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N|summary=Eclipse Jetty is prone to an HTTP header vulnerability.|insight=Jetty accepts the '+' character proceeding the content-length value in a HTTP/1 header field. This is more permissive than allowed by the RFC and other servers routinely reject such requests with 400 responses. There is no known exploit scenario, but it is conceivable that request smuggling could result if jetty is used in combination with a server that does not close the connection after sending such a 400 response.|affected=Eclipse Jetty version 9.0.0 through 9.4.51, 10.0.0 through 10.0.15, 11.0.0 through 11.0.15 and version 12.0.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.4.52, 10.0.16, 11.0.16, 12.0.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.2.13.20150730 Fixed version: 9.4.52 Installation path / port: 8161/tcp

result.name: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information via HTTP

nvt.name: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information via HTTP
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:A/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:N|summary=The host / application transmits sensitive information (username, passwords) in cleartext via HTTP.|insight=|affected=Hosts / applications which doesn't enforce the transmission of sensitive data via an encrypted SSL/TLS connection.|impact=An attacker could use this situation to compromise or eavesdrop on the HTTP communication between the client and the server using a man-in-the-middle attack to get access to sensitive data like usernames or passwords.|solution=Enforce the transmission of sensitive data via an encrypted SSL/TLS connection. Additionally make sure the host / application is redirecting all users to the secured SSL/TLS connection before allowing to input sensitive data into the mentioned functions.|vuldetect=Evaluate previous collected information and check if the host / application is not enforcing the transmission of sensitive data via an encrypted SSL/TLS connection.
The script is currently checking the following:
- HTTP Basic Authentication (Basic Auth)
- HTTP Forms (e.g. Login) with input field of type 'password'|solution_type=Workaround
nvt.solution: Enforce the transmission of sensitive data via an encrypted SSL/TLS connection. Additionally make sure the host / application is redirecting all users to the secured SSL/TLS connection before allowing to input sensitive data into the mentioned functions.

result.name: Eclipse Jetty Session Vulnerability (GHSA-m6cp-vxjx-65j6) - Linux

nvt.name: Eclipse Jetty Session Vulnerability (GHSA-m6cp-vxjx-65j6) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:P/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=Eclipse Jetty is prone to a vulnerability in the session management.|insight=If an exception is thrown from the SessionListener#sessionDestroyed() method, then the session ID is not invalidated in the session ID manager. On deployments with clustered sessions and multiple contexts this can result in a session not being invalidated. This can result in an application used on a shared computer being left logged in.|affected=Eclipse Jetty version 9.4.40.v20210413 and prior, 10.x through 10.0.2 and 11.x through 11.0.2.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.4.41.v20210516, 10.0.3, 11.0.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.2.13.20150730 Fixed version: 9.4.41.20210516 Installation path / port: 8161/tcp

result.name: Eclipse Jetty XXE Vulnerability (GHSA-58qw-p7qm-5rvh) - Linux

nvt.name: Eclipse Jetty XXE Vulnerability (GHSA-58qw-p7qm-5rvh) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:H/Au:M/C:P/I:P/A:P|summary=Eclipse Jetty is prone to a XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability in the XMLParser.|insight=XmlParser is being used when parsing Jetty's xml configuration files. An attacker might exploit this vulnerability in order to achieve SSRF or cause a denial of service.
One possible scenario is importing a (remote) malicious WAR into a Jetty's server, while the WAR includes a malicious web.xml.|affected=Eclipse Jetty version 9.4.51 and prior, 10.0.0 through 10.0.15 and 11.0.0 through 11.0.15.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.4.52, 10.0.16, 11.0.16 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.2.13.20150730 Fixed version: 9.4.52 Installation path / port: 8161/tcp

result.name: Eclipse Jetty CgiServlet Vulnerability (GHSA-3gh6-v5v9-6v9j) - Linux

nvt.name: Eclipse Jetty CgiServlet Vulnerability (GHSA-3gh6-v5v9-6v9j) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N|summary=Eclipse Jetty is prone to a vulnerability in the CgiServlet.|insight=Users of the CgiServlet with a very specific command structure may have the wrong command executed. If a user sends a request to a org.eclipse.jetty.servlets.CGI Servlet for a binary with a space in its name, the servlet will escape the command by wrapping it in quotation marks. This wrapped command, plus an optional command prefix, will then be executed through a call to Runtime.exec. If the original binary name provided by the user contains a quotation mark followed by a space, the resulting command line will contain multiple tokens instead of one.|affected=Eclipse Jetty version 9.0.0 through 9.4.51, 10.0.0 through 10.0.15 and 11.0.0 through 11.0.15.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.4.52, 10.0.16, 11.0.16 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.2.13.20150730 Fixed version: 9.4.52 Installation path / port: 8161/tcp

result.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure

nvt.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.|insight=The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as defined by RFC1323/RFC7323.|affected=TCP implementations that implement RFC1323/RFC7323.|impact=A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.|solution=To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.|vuldetect=Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.

result.description:
It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 1892358772 Packet 2: 1892359859

result.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.|insight=The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.|affected=|impact=This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.|solution=Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)|vuldetect=Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)

result.description: The following response / ICMP packet has been received: - ICMP Type: 14 - ICMP Code: 0

After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=
